summary.intent: Add slash command support (e.g. /new, /compact, /help) to the Matrix channel, which was the only channel missing this feature that all other channels already had.
summary.outcome: Implemented slash command interception in `crates/matrix/src/handler.rs` with a HELP_TEXT constant, command dispatch via `sink.dispatch_command()`, and two unit tests. The change compiles clean with passing tests and is committed and ready to push as a PR.

repo: All channels (WhatsApp, Teams, Telegram, Discord, Slack) share a common gateway `dispatch_command()` dispatcher — channel-specific handlers just need to intercept the slash prefix and delegate to it.
The simplest slash command interception pattern (used by WhatsApp and Teams) is a plain `body.strip_prefix('/')` text check, no protocol-specific command parsing needed.
Matrix handler processes messages in `handle_room_message`; the insertion point for command interception is right before `sink.dispatch_to_chat()` inside the `if let Some(sink) = &event_sink` block.
Telegram implements a local `/help` handler returning a HELP_TEXT constant rather than dispatching to the gateway — Matrix follows this same pattern.
The project uses a pinned nightly Rust toolchain for formatting; `cargo fmt` must be run with the nightly toolchain to avoid format drift.
Clippy failures from CUDA crate are pre-existing and unrelated to channel code changes.

code.finding: Text message dispatch in handle_room_message — slash command check must be inserted before `sink.dispatch_to_chat()` on ~line 376, after the location coordinate check.
HELP_TEXT constant added here listing all 12 slash commands; placed near top of file after existing constants.
Command interception block added: checks strip_prefix('/'), handles /help locally, delegates all other commands to sink.dispatch_command(), sends response via send_text().

workflow: Issues were created and claimed in a tracker (beads) before implementation, and closed after completion — issue lifecycle is managed via CLI tooling.
Integration test coverage for command dispatch is provided by the gateway's existing dispatch_command tests; only unit tests for constants and detection logic were added at the crate level.
Running clippy scoped to the modified crate (`-p matrix`) avoids noise from pre-existing failures in unrelated crates.

friction: Multiple re-reads of handler.rs were needed (at least 5 separate reads) to locate the exact insertion point, suggesting the file is large and the relevant section wasn't immediately obvious.
Clippy run on the full workspace produced unrelated CUDA failures that had to be identified as pre-existing before scoping the check to just the matrix crate.
open_items: PR was staged for push/creation at end of session but the transcript ends before confirming the PR was actually created.
The 12 commands listed in HELP_TEXT should be kept in sync if new commands are added to other channels — no automated enforcement of this consistency exists.
